result: If I could change the world in one way, it would be to instill a universal sense of empathy in every person. Empathy—the ability to truly understand and share the feelings of others—could bridge divides, reduce conflict, and foster cooperation on a global scale. It would mean people would naturally consider the impact of their actions on others, whether in personal interactions, policy decisions, or environmental choices. Wars might be less likely when leaders feel the pain of those they oppose. Inequality could shrink if the fortunate genuinely felt the struggles of the less privileged. Even environmental destruction might slow if humans could viscerally sense the suffering of ecosystems. It’s not a perfect fix—empathy can be messy and overwhelming—but it’s a foundation for a world that prioritizes connection over division. That’s the change I’d make.
